summary_en: Year-end dividend for FY3/26 raised from 25 yen to 38 yen per share (+13 yen, +52%). Full-year dividend now 57 yen (prior forecast 44 yen). Increase reflects consolidated earnings disclosed 14 May 2026 and shareholder-return policy targeting total payout ratio of 50%+ (dividends plus buybacks).
title_en: Notice on Revision to FY3/26 Year-End Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase)
translation_en_brief:
PCI Holdings announced on 14 May 2026 that its board has resolved to revise the FY3/26 year-end dividend forecast upward. The year-end dividend per share is raised from the previously forecast 25 yen to 38 yen, an increase of 13 yen or 52%. This brings the total annual dividend to 57 yen (the interim dividend remains at 19 yen; prior full-year forecast was 44 yen).

The company states that shareholder profit return is a top management priority. The revision reflects consolidated earnings results disclosed in the same announcement on 14 May 2026. The board is following a fundamental policy of ensuring financial soundness and adequate internal reserves while pursuing stable and continuous dividends plus active share buybacks, with a target total payout ratio of 50% or above. The increased dividend is grounded in improved operating performance.
